quotes.text: Everyone thinks the whispering on the first album was this intense artistic choice. Really, Finneas was just asleep in the next room and I didn't want him to wake up and eat my cereal.
quotes.speaker: Billie Eilish
quotes.fake_note: Billie and her brother Finneas famously recorded their debut in his bedroom, making this sibling dynamic sound very real. However, the whispering was a deliberate vocal styling, not a cereal defense strategy.

quotes.text: My topic is the shift from 'architect' to 'gardener', where 'architect' stands for 'someone who carries a full picture of the work before it is made', to 'gardener' standing for 'someone who plants seeds and waits to see exactly what will come up'.
quotes.speaker: Brian Eno
quotes.context: Composer/producer Brian Eno (Roxy Music, ambient music pioneer, producer for Bowie, Talking Heads, U2) framing how he thinks about making music — not as an architect with the finished work in his head, but as a gardener who plants seeds and

source.title: Composers as Gardeners — Brian Eno, Edge.org

quotes.text: I refuse to use modern guitar picks. If it's not carved from a melted-down 1930s Bakelite radio dial, you are fundamentally disrespecting the frequency of the blues.
quotes.speaker: Jack White
quotes.fake_note: White's legendary obsession with vintage, analog equipment and strict self-imposed rules makes this rigid mandate sound completely true. The giveaway is the absurdity of melting down antique radios just to strum a guitar.

quotes.text: It’s kind of ironic that there is a British guy a part of it

quotes.text: I spent three weeks trying to make a synthesizer sound exactly like dust settling on velvet. We eventually just recorded the velvet.
quotes.speaker: Brian Eno
quotes.fake_note: Eno is famous for his extreme ambient experimentation and esoteric recording techniques, making this sound exactly like his creative process. The tell is the impossible acoustics of recording 'dust.'

quotes.text: Martha showed me how to perfectly fold a fitted sheet, and in return I taught her how to make a pancake that looks exactly like a basset hound. It’s an exchange of cultures.
quotes.speaker: Snoop Dogg
quotes.fake_note: Snoop and Martha Stewart's real-life friendship is full of endearing, domestic moments, making this trade of skills highly plausible. But Snoop's pancake artistry is pure wholesome fiction.
